findings.issue: Task 15 (Test Audit) describes auditing 9 test files, but only 1 of the 9 exists at execution time: ChatViewModelTest.kt. The other 8 (AutoTitleGeneratorTest, SettingsMigrationHelperTest, ChatRepositoryTest, WarmupCoordinatorTest, ModelRegistryActiveModelTest, ChatDaoTest, MessageDaoTest, SanctumDatabaseTest) are not yet created — they are outputs of Tasks 1–8. If Task 15 runs before those tasks complete, the audit is impossible for 8/9 files.
findings.fix: The depends_on: [12] only ensures docs are updated. Task 15 must also transitively depend on all code tasks (1–11) that produce the test files. Either expand depends_on to include all code tasks, or document explicitly in the task description that the auditor must verify all 9 files exist before starting and treat absent files as a critical gap finding. Wave 7 placement implies code tasks are done, but the depends_on declaration does not enforce this — consider adding depends_on: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 12] to make the dependency explicit.

findings.issue: Task 15 'Details' section prescribes exactly what each test file 'should cover' with itemized bullet lists that read as a spec for writing tests, not hints for auditing them. For example: 'AutoTitleGeneratorTest (pure function, no mocks needed): trim → whitespace collapse → cut at last space ≤ 20 →

suffix / No cut needed when text is exactly 20 chars / Cut falls on a space boundary...' — this is a step-by-step enumeration of test cases. An audit task should describe what coverage gaps to look for, not prescribe the implementation of the test suite.
findings.fix: Rephrase the Details section to be audit-oriented: 'Verify that AutoTitleGeneratorTest covers the following AC-U2 branches: ...' rather than 'AutoTitleGeneratorTest should have:'. The current form crosses the line from hints into prescribed implementation.

findings.issue: Task 16 Verification Steps (static verification block, step 8) contains a grep comment that says '# Step 8: ErrorLog whitelist — expect 7 lines (6 Phase-3 components + settings-io)' but the actual grep pattern matches all 7 strings from a combined OR pattern. The 'expect 7 lines' expectation is fragile: each component string may appear more than once in the file (e.g. in a comment and in the set). The Details section correctly says to grep for each of the 14 components individually, contradicting the combined-pattern approach in the code block.
findings.fix: Replace the single combined grep in the verification code block with 14 individual greps (one per expected component), or rewrite as a count-based check against the ALLOWED_COMPONENTS set definition specifically. Remove the '— expect 7 lines' comment since it will produce false failures if any component string appears more than once.

findings.issue: Step 10 (privacy manifest) in the Verification Steps static-check code block uses 'aapt dump xmltree app-debug.apk ...' (no path prefix). The APK is produced at app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk. The command as written will fail with 'file not found' unless the agent cds to the output directory. The Details section mentions the correct path workaround but the code example still has the bug.
findings.fix: Update the aapt command in the verification block to: 'aapt dump xmltree app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk AndroidManifest.xml | grep -E "allowBackup|dataExtractionRules"'

findings.issue: Task 15 step 11 (TDD compliance) says to run 'git log --diff-filter=A --name-only ... -- app/src/test/...' but uses a bare path prefix rather than quoted glob. On some shells this pattern may not expand correctly without quotes. The command also uses '...' (Unix) style paths which is correct for bash but worth confirming since the repo is on Windows with a bash shell.
findings.fix: Minor: confirm the git log path pattern works in bash on Windows. The task's verification command block uses Unix-style forward slashes which is correct for bash on Windows — no change needed beyond awareness.
